title: uniname
text:
uniname Return the name of a component, instance, operation, parameter, entity, or entity field. Explain this code long uniname (UHACThAny, intseqNr, intmaxLen, UCHAR*name); Copy Parameters hAny Handle of an instance, operation, parameter, or entity. seqNr Sequence number of the parameter or entity field. maxLen Maximum size of the name to be returned. name Requested name. This buffer must be at least as large as the value of maxLen . (Output). Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uniname Value Meaning 0 Success. -57 Instance was not found. -165 The supplied handle is not valid. -1203 The sequence number is out of range. -1406 Memory could not be allocated or freed. Use Library: ucall Description uniname returns the name of an instance, component, operation, parameter, or entity field, depending on the type of handle that was supplied in hAny . For a parameter or entity field, the sequence number must be supplied in parameter seqNr (which must be between 1 and the number of parameters or fields, inclusive). When the supplied handle is an instance handle, you can set seqNr to 0 to get the instance name (if any), or to 1 to get the name of the component. Example: uoprprms, uniname, uprmdir, and uprmtype The following routine prints out a list of the parameters of an arbitrary operation in an arbitrary component. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> char *types[] = { "Unknown", "String", "Boolean", "Numeric", "Float", "Date", "Time", "DateTime","LinearDate", "LinearTime", "LinearDateTime", "Raw", "Image", "Entity", "Occurrence", "IncludedEntity", "Handle" }; char *dires[] = { "Unknown", "In", "Out", "InOut" }; void inspect_operation_params (char *svcname, char *oprname) { UHACT hEnv, hIn, hOp, hParm; long i, nParams; int pType, pDir; char name[1+UPRMNAMESIZE]; char* template1 = " %3s %-20s %-20s %s
"; char* template2 = " %3d %-20s %-20s %s
"; ueopen(&hEnv); uinstnew(hEnv, (UCHAR*)svcname, 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, 0); uinstopr(hIn, (UCHAR*)oprname, 0, &hOp); printf("Parameters of operation %s in service %s:

", oprname, svcname); printf(template1, "Num", "Name ", "Type ", "Direction"); printf(template1, "===", "====================", "====================", "========="); uoprprms(hOp, &nParams); for ( i=1; i<=nParams; i++ ) { uprmdir(hOp, i, &pDir); uprmgeth(hOp, i, 0, &hParm); uprmtype(hParm, &pType); uniname(hOp, i, UPRMNAMESIZE, name); printf(template2, i, name, types[pType], dires[pDir]); } printf("
"); uinstdel(hIn, 0); ufreeh(&hOp); ufreeh(&hIn); ufreeh(&hEnv); } Copy Here is an example of output generated by this function: Explain this code Parameters of operation MYOP in service TESTSVC: Num Name Type Direction === ==================== ==================== ========= 1 P1 Numeric In 2 P2 String InOut 3 P3 Date Out Copy Related Links uoprprms Return the number of parameters in the specified operation. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
